plain: an address only THIS machine can reach; nothing off the host can connect
why: the current report server binds loopback only (serve.py:19), so it needs SSH forwarding and can't back a public domain

plain: a stable per-plugin data folder ~/.claude/plugins/data/trainlint-trainlint that survives version upgrades
why: per-project data already lives here (paths.py); reports should too, so one server isn't stranded by a version bump

plain: a small daemon that dials OUT from your host to Cloudflare, so your domain reaches a local port with NO open inbound ports
why: the host's inbound reachability is unknown and no proxy is installed; a tunnel needs zero open ports and gives auto-TLS

plain: a login wall enforced at Cloudflare's edge BEFORE traffic reaches your server, allowing only the emails you list
why: the reports are private; Access gates them to just your email without any password on the origin

plain: the hidden hostname a Cloudflare Tunnel is reached at; your domain CNAMEs to <tunnel-id>.cfargotunnel.com
why: this is the DNS record that points secondfoundationlabs.com at the tunnel

plain: the current server only checks 'is something already on port 8420?' before starting — not WHICH folder that something serves
why: that's the stale-dir bug: an old server on 8420 keeps serving an old version's folder

plain: the actual local web server (python -m http.server) the tunnel forwards to
why: cloudflared/Access sit in front of it; the origin just serves static report HTML on loopback

plain: one service that hosts many separate users, each seeing only their own data
why: the pivot: this now hosts reports for every plugin installer, not just you

plain: code that runs on Cloudflare's edge (no server of your own) to handle web requests
why: it's the entire server side here -- upload, index, and serving reports

plain: Cloudflare's S3-like object storage for files/blobs
why: stores each tenant's report HTML, keyed by their namespace

plain: a per-user prefix (from their email) that all their stored reports live under
why: the isolation boundary -- the Worker only ever reads/writes the caller's namespace

plain: a signed string the Worker mints for a logged-in user, that the plugin uses to upload without a browser
why: it binds a machine upload to the same tenant as the human's Access login -- the load-bearing piece

plain: an edge login wall; each user signs in with Google/GitHub and Access tells the Worker who they are
why: handles login for ALL tenants once, with no per-installer infra
